title: Backlog
description: First-class backlog inside a venture (Opportunity Management v2.1). 'backlogs/' sits at the SAME level as 'teams/'; a backlog is not owned by a team but may reference one via teamRef (N:1, optional -> may be shared or team-less). A 'discovery' backlog collects ideas; a 'delivery' backlog collects epics/use-cases. This schema holds the backlog's own metadata + membership; the node/link graph projection is a derived GraphView document (graph-view.schema.json, '*.glens.json', #011) pointed to by graphRef.

tenantId.description: Reserved for the future multi-tenant SaaS layer; null in the local file-based store.

workspaceId.description: Reserved workspace scope for the future SaaS layer; null in the local store.

slug.description: Filesystem-friendly slug, also the '<slug>.backlog.json' filename stem.

kind.description: Backlog kind. 'discovery' members are ideas; 'delivery' members are epics/use-cases.

teamRef.description: Optional id of the owning team (team.schema.json, N:1). Null when the backlog is shared or team-less; a team is reached indirectly (epic -> backlog -> team).

memberRefs.description: Ids of the entities that belong to this backlog: ideas for a 'discovery' backlog; epics and/or use-cases for a 'delivery' backlog.

relations.description: Relations between members, mirrored into the derived GraphView projection. 'rel' reuses the GraphView relation vocabulary plus 'implements' (use-case -> idea).

rel.description: Relation kind (GraphView link.rel vocabulary extended with 'implements').

graphRef.description: Optional relative path to the derived GraphView projection ('<slug>.glens.json') for this backlog.
